title: `skopeo copy` to local file-system doesn't appear to store signatures
body:
Using `skopeo` `1.3.1-dev` on Ubuntu `20.04` or `1.3.0` on macOS `11.4`, I don't appear to be storing signatures when using `skopeo copy` to copy *from* Docker Hub *to* the local file-system.

In both cases, I'm running: -

`skopeo --debug copy --all docker://davidhay1969/hello_world_nginx_dct:latest dir:///tmp/hello_world_nginx_dct`

On the Ubuntu box, this returns: -

```text

DEBU[0000] overwriting existing container image directory "/tmp/hello_world_nginx_dct" 
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf"

DEBU[0000] Trying to access "docker.io/davidhay1969/hello_world_nginx_dct:latest" 
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for docker.io in credential helper containers-auth.json 
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration 
DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration        
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/davidhay1969/hello_world_nginx_dct:latest, using built-in default file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore 
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/docker.io

DEBU[0001] Overall: allowed                             
Getting image source signatures
DEBU[0001] Reading /var/lib/containers/sigstore/davidhay1969/hello_world_nginx_dct@sha256=0de773124007c92b3bc8f00052a76d3568247e0806a2306bce12930e64d0c839/signature-1 
DEBU[0001] Downloading /v2/davidhay1969/hello_world_nginx_dct/blobs/sha256:7184c046fdf17da4c16ca482e5ede36e1f2d41ac8cea9c036e488fd149d6e8e7

DEBU[0026] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/davidhay1969/hello_world_nginx_dct/blobs/sha256:7c64cf4d0e3dfde1067f35995eaf182f0d22d74f80b854f0e52e91c6b81e20f8 
DEBU[0026] No compression detected                      
DEBU[0026] Using original blob without modification     
Copying config 7c64cf4d0e done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

However, in both cases, I don't appear to have anything stored in the signature store: -

**Ubuntu**

`ls -al /var/lib/containers/sigstore/`

```text
total 0
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root  6 Jun 16 06:40 .
drwxr-xr-x 5 root root 50 Jun 24 11:47 ..
```

**macOS**

`ls -al /Users/hayd/.local/share/containers/sigstore`

```text
total 0
drwxr-xr-x  2 hayd  staff   64 25 Jun 16:49 .
drwx------  4 hayd  staff  128 25 Jun 16:49 ..
```

( I even created the `sigstore` subdirectory under `~/.local/share/containers` but to no avail )

For the record, this image was built/pushed using Docker Content Trust enabled: -

```bash
export DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1
DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST_SERVER=https://docker.io:4443
docker build -t davidhay1969/hello_world_nginx_dct_june_2021:latest -f Dockerfile .
docker push davidhay1969/hello_world_nginx_dct_june_2021:latest
```

As ever, this is probably pilot error but ... 🤷

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Yeah, the Skopeo “simple signing” is not Docker Content Trust, and does not interoperate in any way. See maybe https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/docs/tutorials/image_signing.md .

Hey @mtrmac ah, OK, that helps - I'd misundstood that point 😢 

So there's probably not much point my trying to use `skopeo standalone-verify` as that'd require me to have the signature for the image that had previously been signed using DCT ...
To be explicit, `standalone-verify` also can’t do anything with the artifacts created by DCT.

Right, thanks @mtrmac that's really useful intel - does it make sense to add some commentary to that effect to the documentation: -

[skopeo-standalone-verify](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/docs/skopeo-standalone-verify.1.md)

or is the phrase `standalone` good enough to indicate that ?

Happy to PR a comment in, if that'd help others ?

That’s a good point, nothing in the repository says anything.

Focusing on the more generally-applicable `skopeo copy`, that should _definitely_ say something about the signature format 
used (maybe a NOTES section near the end , pointing at https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-signature.5.md ), and a PR would be very welcome. 

---

WRT `standalone-verify`, I think that adding a

> This is primarily a debugging tool, or useful for special cases,
> and usually should not be a part of your normal operational workflow

warning like `standalone-sign` might be a bit more helpful to direct users to the right tooling; OTOH pointing at the right format wouldn’t hurt either, of course.
Certainly, will raise a PR over the weekend, and attach it to this issue ....

@mtrmac apologies for the delay in getting around to this, have raised PR #1355 - hope this is in line with your expectations. All comments gratefully received

PS Was able to use `skopeo copy` and `skopeo standalone-verify` with OpenPGP keys 🥂
